hero.subtitle: Learn the digital skills you need to succeed, step by step.

landing.info: Master the digital world with free, offline lessons designed for you.
landing.heading: Learn Digital Skills In Your Language
landing.subtitle: Empowering young people in Northern Nigeria with free, offline digital literacy.

phone_basics.title: Phone Basics
phone_basics.description: Learn the basic parts of your phone and how to use them confidently.

internet_101.title: Internet 101
internet_101.description: Understand how to browse safely and use search engines.

email_messaging.title: Email and Messaging
email_messaging.description: Create a Gmail account and use WhatsApp effectively.

online_safety.title: Online Safety
online_safety.description: Protect your personal information and recognize scams.

errors.no_account: No account found with that number. Please register first.

lessons: Create strong passwords and keep them private
Spot online scams and fake offers
Protect your phone with lock screen and updates
Share safely on WhatsApp and social media
What the internet is and how it works
Use search engines to find trusted information
Open websites safely and avoid harmful links
Manage mobile data and browse efficiently

step1.heading: Meet your home screen
step1.content: Your home screen is the main place on your phone. You can open apps from here.

step2.heading: Use touch actions
step2.content: Tap to open, hold to see options, and swipe to move between screens.

step3.heading: Adjust basic settings
step3.content: Open settings to change brightness, sound, and language so your phone feels easier to use.

step1.heading: Open the phone app
step1.content: Tap the phone icon, type a number, and press call to start speaking.

step2.heading: Answer and end calls
step2.content: When someone calls, swipe or tap answer. Tap the red button to end the call.

step3.heading: Use your contacts
step3.content: Save names with numbers so you can call family and friends faster next time.

step1.heading: Open your messages app
step1.content: Tap messages, then choose a contact or type a number for a new message.

step2.heading: Write and send clearly
step2.content: Keep your message short and clear. Tap send when you are ready.

step3.heading: Check message replies
step3.content: Open the same conversation to read new replies and continue chatting.

step1.heading: Use the app store
step1.content: Open Play Store, search for the app name, and check the app publisher before installing.

step2.heading: Open and organize apps
step2.content: After install, open the app from your home screen and move it to a place you can find quickly.

step3.heading: Remove apps you do not use
step3.content: Uninstall old apps to free space and keep your phone fast.

step1.heading: Save battery power
step1.content: Reduce brightness, turn off data when not needed, and close apps running in the background.

step2.heading: Free up storage
step2.content: Delete duplicate photos, old downloads, and videos you no longer need.

step3.heading: Check phone health
step3.content: Open battery and storage settings often so your phone stays smooth and reliable.

step1.heading: Internet means connection
step1.content: The internet connects phones and computers so people can share information.

step2.heading: Websites and apps use internet
step2.content: When you open YouTube, Google, or WhatsApp, your phone uses internet data.

step3.heading: Data is limited
step3.content: Use data wisely by avoiding unnecessary videos and downloads.

lesson2.title: Connecting to WiFi and mobile data

step1.heading: Connect to WiFi
step1.content: Open settings, choose WiFi, and enter the password to connect.

step2.heading: Use mobile data
step2.content: Turn on mobile data when WiFi is not available. Watch your data balance.

step3.heading: Choose secure networks
step3.content: Avoid unknown public WiFi when entering personal details.

step1.heading: Open your browser
step1.content: Use Chrome or another browser to visit websites.

step2.heading: Type web addresses
step2.content: Enter website names carefully to avoid fake pages.

step3.heading: Use tabs and back button
step3.content: Tabs help you keep multiple pages open. Back button returns to the previous page.

step1.heading: Use simple search words
step1.content: Type clear words like weather Kano today for better results.

step2.heading: Open trusted results
step2.content: Pick results from known sites and avoid suspicious links.

step3.heading: Double-check information
step3.content: Compare information from two or more reliable websites before sharing.

step1.heading: Look for HTTPS
step1.content: Trusted sites usually start with https and show a lock icon.

step2.heading: Check the website name
step2.content: Scam sites use names similar to real brands. Read the full address carefully.

step3.heading: Protect your details
step3.content: Do not enter password or bank details unless you are sure the site is real.

step1.heading: Email is digital post
step1.content: Email lets you send messages and files to people over the internet.

step2.heading: Every email has an address
step2.content: An address like name@gmail.com helps people send messages to you.

step3.heading: Email is useful for school and work
step3.content: You can use email for forms, jobs, and learning opportunities.

step1.heading: Open Gmail sign up
step1.content: Go to Gmail create account and fill your basic details.

step2.heading: Create a strong password
step2.content: Use letters, numbers, and symbols. Do not use your name only.

step3.heading: Add recovery options
step3.content: Add a phone number so you can recover your account if needed.

step1.heading: Compose a new email
step1.content: Tap compose, add recipient address, then write a clear subject and message.

step2.heading: Read inbox messages
step2.content: Open your inbox regularly to read replies and important updates.

step3.heading: Reply politely
step3.content: Use respectful and short replies so communication stays clear.

step1.heading: Set up your profile
step1.content: Add your name and a simple photo so friends can identify you.

step2.heading: Send text and voice notes
step2.content: Use text for quick messages and voice notes when typing is hard.

step3.heading: Manage group chats
step3.content: Mute noisy groups and avoid forwarding unverified messages.

step1.heading: Protect private chats
step1.content: Do not share personal photos, codes, or sensitive details with strangers.

step2.heading: Spot fake links
step2.content: Ignore links promising free money, gifts, or urgent account fixes.

step3.heading: Block and report abuse
step3.content: Use block and report tools if someone threatens or scams you.

step1.heading: Know what is private
step1.content: Phone number, ID details, and bank information should be kept private.

step2.heading: Share less online
step2.content: Only share what is needed. Too much personal posting can be risky.

step3.heading: Use privacy settings
step3.content: Limit who can see your profile, posts, and status updates.

step1.heading: Watch for urgent pressure
step1.content: Scammers push you to act fast. Slow down and verify first.

step2.heading: Check the sender
step2.content: Look closely at numbers and email addresses before trusting messages.

step3.heading: Do not share OTP codes
step3.content: One-time password codes are private. No real support team will ask for them.

step1.heading: Use long passwords
step1.content: Use at least 10 characters with mixed letters, numbers, and symbols.

step2.heading: Use different passwords
step2.content: Do not use one password for every app. If one leaks, others stay safe.

step3.heading: Store passwords safely
step3.content: Use a trusted password manager or a private notebook kept secure.

step1.heading: Think before posting
step1.content: Ask if this post is safe and respectful before sharing publicly.

step2.heading: Control who contacts you
step2.content: Use privacy controls to reduce unknown messages and harassment.

step3.heading: Avoid harmful challenges
step3.content: Do not join dangerous online trends just because others are doing them.

lesson5.title: What to do if something goes wrong

step1.heading: Act quickly
step1.content: Change password immediately if you think your account was accessed.

step2.heading: Ask for trusted help
step2.content: Contact a trusted friend, family member, or official support channel.

step3.heading: Report and recover
step3.content: Use report tools in the app and follow recovery steps to secure your account.
